headline: MONSTER HIT: Trident Drills 15.11 g/t Au Over 51.8m at Ex-Cameco Saskatchewan Gold Site — Stock Surges 19%
snippet: Trident Resources (TSXV: ROCK) hit 51.83 metres grading 15.11 g/t Au from 256m depth at its Contact Lake project in northern Saskatchewan — a former Cameco-operated gold mine shuttered when gold was $300/oz. The interval includes a 0.5m bonanza of 1,055 g/t gold; shares jumped 19% on the news with $30M in treasury fully funding 2026.

headline: USGS: 2.3 MILLION TONNES Under the Appalachians — Enough Lithium for 130M EVs and 328 Years of US Supply
snippet: A new USGS assessment identified approximately 2.3 million metric tons of undiscovered, potentially recoverable lithium in Appalachian pegmatites spanning the Carolinas, Maine, and New Hampshire — equivalent to replacing 328 years of US import levels. Resources are largely undrilled, representing an early-stage strategic opportunity under the domestic supply push.

headline: GOLD SLIDES to $4,500 One-Month Low — Iran Talks Stall, Hormuz Closure Fuels Inflation Fears Ahead of Fed
snippet: Spot gold fell nearly 2% to ~$4,510/oz on April 29, its lowest level since late March, as stalled US-Iran peace talks and the ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure drive inflation expectations higher ahead of the Federal Reserve's next policy decision. Gold remains up 38% year-on-year despite the pullback.

headline: 1,385 g/t SILVER: Argenta's El Quevar Returns Eye-Popping Intercept — New High-Grade Zone Found 800m From Resource
snippet: Argenta Silver (TSXV: AGAG) intercepted 1,385 g/t Ag over 4.0 metres at the Mani-Copan target at El Quevar in Argentina, confirming a new high-grade silver zone 800m from the edge of its existing 45.3 Moz resource grading 482 g/t. The company has upsized its drill program to 25,000 metres on the strength of results.

headline: MINI BRE-X: RCMP Charges Former RPX Gold CEO for Allegedly Inflating Resources by Up to 87,000 oz
snippet: Quentin Yarie, former CEO of RPX Gold (formerly Red Pine Exploration, TSXV), was charged April 28 with fraud and forgery after RCMP alleged he manipulated assay data to overstate gold resources by 62,000–87,000 ounces in the company's 2023 technical report. The Toronto Integrated Market Enforcement Team probe began in 2024 after RPX disclosed drilling irregularities.

headline: Anglo American Posts Q1 Copper Rise — But China's Acid Ban Keeps Chile Supply Chain Threat Alive Into May
snippet: Anglo American reported a 1% increase in Q1 copper output from its Chilean Los Bronces and Collahuasi operations, maintaining its full-year production outlook. The company faces looming supply risks as China's sulphuric acid export ban — effective May 1 — threatens to cut Chilean copper refining capacity, with spot acid prices hitting $400/tonne vs a $150 long-run forecast.

headline: 1.46Moz Gold at Duquesne West — Emperor Metals Hits 35.2 g/t Over 21.7m in Quebec
snippet: Emperor Metals updated its Duquesne West resource to 1.46 million ounces at 1.69 g/t Au, anchored by drill hole DQ26-06 returning 21.7 metres at 35.2 g/t Au from 190m depth. The company's 23,000-metre 2026 campaign in Quebec — including 8,000m of historical core resampling — is already delivering high-grade headline numbers.

headline: LUCA DRILLS 118m AT 2.5 g/t AuEq: Polymetallic VMS Hit Expands Footprint at Campo Morado, Mexico
snippet: Luca Mining (TSXV: LUCA) intersected 118 metres grading 2.5 g/t Au, 78 g/t Ag, 0.8% Cu, 0.6% Pb, and 2.0% Zn from 180m at the unmined Largo Norte zone at Campo Morado, Mexico. A second hole returned 100.7m at 2.80 g/t Au from 113m, extending the zone and confirming near-mine scale mineralization at a producing polymetallic mine.

headline: CHINA BETS $1.7B ON ECUADOR: CMOC Signs Deal for Los Cangrejos — Country's Largest Gold-Copper Project
snippet: Ecuador has signed a mining contract with China's CMOC Group for the Los Cangrejos project, with total expected investment exceeding $1.7 billion and the state retaining a 50% revenue share plus $54M in advance royalties. The open-pit gold-copper mine will be Ecuador's largest, with construction targeted before end-2027 and projected state revenue of $4.39B over the mine's life.

headline: BARRICK ADVANCES NORTH AMERICA IPO: Taps Goldman Sachs, Names Executive Team for Year-End Listing
snippet: Barrick Mining has appointed the executive leadership team for its standalone North American gold business and tapped Goldman Sachs to lead an IPO targeting completion by year-end 2026, with a primary NYSE listing and secondary TSX listing planned. The spin-out is seen by analysts as a catalyst to unlock value after years of Barrick trading at a discount to its gold peers.

headline: CARTEL GOLD at the US MINT: NYT Investigation Exposes Colombian Drug Money in American Eagle Supply Chain
snippet: A New York Times investigation found the US Mint — which sells over $1 billion in gold coins annually — sourced gold from mines controlled by Colombia's Gulf Clan cartel for two decades without auditing upstream provenance. Treasury Secretary Bessent has pledged a full review of the Mint's procurement practices.

headline: RARE EARTH MOMENTUM: Aclara Surges 14% as US-China Supply Standoff and Project Vault Accelerate Sector
snippet: Rare earth equities gained broad momentum as pressure to build China-independent supply chains intensified. Aclara Resources (TSX: ARA) surged 14% while the sector's eyes are on USA Rare Earth's $2.8B Serra Verde acquisition and Trump's Project Vault — a $10B Export-Import Bank critical minerals reserve initiative targeting non-Chinese rare earth supply.
